subtopics.title: Definition and Importance of Food
common_misconceptions: Food and nutrition are the same
Skipping meals helps with weight loss
subtopics.teaching_note: Food = any substance consumed to provide nutritional support. Nutrition = science of how food affects the body. Nutrients = chemical compounds in food used by the body. Balanced diet contains all nutrients in correct proportions. Nigerian context: Common staples: garri, rice, yam, plantain, beans. Protein sources: fish, meat, eggs, beans, soybeans. Vegetables: ugu, efo, bitter leaf. Fruits: mango, pawpaw, pineapple, orange. Malnutrition: kwashiorkor (protein deficiency), marasmus (calorie deficiency).

subtopics.title: Macronutrients
common_misconceptions: Protein is only for building muscles
subtopics.teaching_note: Carbohydrates: main energy source. 1g = 4 kcal. Sources: rice, yam, garri, bread, cereals. Simple carbs: sugars. Complex carbs: starch, fibre. Proteins: body building and repair. 1g = 4 kcal. Sources: beans, fish, meat, eggs, soybeans. Complete proteins contain all essential amino acids (animal sources). Fats: energy storage, insulation, hormone production. 1g = 9 kcal. Saturated (animal fats, solid at room temp) vs unsaturated (plant oils, liquid at room temp). Trans fats should be avoided.

subtopics.title: Vitamins and Minerals
common_misconceptions: Minerals and vitamins are the same
subtopics.teaching_note: Vitamins: organic compounds needed in small amounts. Fat-soluble: A, D, E, K (stored in body). Water-soluble: B-complex, C (not stored, need daily). Key vitamins: A (vision, sources: liver, carrots), B1 (beriberi prevention), C (immunity, scurvy prevention, sources: citrus), D (bone health, rickets prevention, from sunlight). Minerals: inorganic. Calcium (bones, teeth, sources: milk), Iron (blood, anaemia prevention, sources: liver, green vegetables), Iodine (thyroid, goitre prevention, sources: iodized salt). Nigerian diet: vitamin A deficiency common - golden rice and vitamin A fortification programs.
